Dahnert et al. propose a randomized assignment to address the challenge of instance-channel ambiguity
The instance-channel mapping function proposed by Dahnert et al. utilizes a randomized assignment to assign instance ids to different channels of voxel features.
The instance-channel ambiguity arises when an instance id may be assigned to an arbitrary channel, which can confuse the 3D refinement model.
The voxel reconstruction ambiguity occurs during the 2D-to-3D lifting process when depth from a single view only propagates 2D information onto the frontal surface in the camera frustum.
The object regions behind the frontal surface pose a challenge for the 3D refinement model to accurately reconstruct.

In this paper, we propose a bottom-up framework with occupancy-aware lifting (BUOL) for panoptic 3D scene reconstruction
BUOL is designed to address the ambiguity in voxel-reconstruction by leveraging multi-plane occupancy predicted by a 2D model.
BUOL leverages occupancy-aware lifting to improve 3D reconstruction and perception.
BUOL outperforms state-of-art approaches through its top-down framework for 3D reconstruction and perception.
BUOL is believed to drive the area of panoptic 3D scene reconstruction from a single image forward.
